homepage.h1: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
homepage.meta_description: Welcome to the website of Suella Bravrman MP.  Reform UK Member of Parliament for Fareham and Waterlooville
homepage.text_excerpt: --> Home | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Suella Braverman KC MP Suella is proud to be the Reform UK Member of Parliament for Fareham and Waterlooville. I was born and raised with a strong sense of duty to our country and the belief that Britain should be a place where hard work, honesty and common sense are rewarded. ​ Having served as Attorney General and Home Secretary, I’ve seen first-hand how Westminster and Whitehall too often forget the people they’re meant to serve. That’s why I joined Reform UK, to put the public back in charge, restore integrity to politics, and make Britain work again for ordinary people. Reform UK offers the hope and change that this country needs to once again be a place we can be proud of. ​ My team and I are here to help. Whether you need support securing action from local services, want advice starting a business or community initiative, or wish to share your views on national issues or proposed legislation, we’re ready to listen and act As your MP in Fareham and Waterlooville, I believe politics should be approachable, practical and honest. That’s why I’ve hosted and attended hundreds of community events to support local families and businesses to give residents a direct voice. ​ If you’d like to meet me in person, please request a surgery appointment. You’re also very welcome to contact my office by telephone, letter or email. My team and I will always do our best to help. ​ Suella ​ Suella Braverman KC MP Reform UK Member of Parliament for Fareham & Waterlooville Learn more Email * Yes, subscribe me to your newsletter. * Submit © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy bottom of page

headings: h2: Scrap the Family Farm Tax
h2: Ending DEI at the FA
h2: Tea and cake in Denmead
pages.text_excerpt: --> News | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Join Suella's email list and receive her latest news and monthly newsletter straight to your inbox. Enter your email here Sign Up London Road HMO 13th April 2026 ​ ​ I’m very concerned by the decision of the Labour‑Lib Dem‑Green‑run Havant Borough Council to approve a 12-bed HMO planning application in Waterlooville. It has not been ruled out that this could be used for asylum seekers, and it’s another example of local politicians putting ideology before the interests of residents. They should never have approved it, and local people have every right to feel frustrated and ignored. ​ Well done and thank you to Reform UK Councillor Gwen Robinson for standing up for our community and opposing this damaging proposal. Gwen represents exactly what local politics should be about: integrity, accountability, and a genuine commitment to putting residents first. ​ On May 7th, you have the chance to send a clear message that enough is enough. It’s time to bring back common sense, competence, and courage to Havant Borough Council. Vote Reform to put local people, not party politics, back in charge! Happy Easter! 5th April 2026 ​ We are so lucky to have some truly beautiful churches here in Fareham and Waterlooville, places of peace, reflection, and community. ​ Easter is always a special time to come together with family and friends, to celebrate hope, renewal, and faith. It’s also a reminder of our country’s deep Christian roots with traditions that have shaped our values, guided our institutions, and given us a shared sense of purpose for centuries. ​ I’m proud that Britain is a Christian country and that our churches continue to play such a vital role in local life, bringing people together, supporting those in need, and keeping faith alive for generations to come. ​ I Hope you all had a wonderful Easter surrounded by loved ones! Hopscotch in Fareham! 2nd April 2026 ​ Very excited that Hopscotch Day Nurseries will be opening a new nursery in Fareham at the old Ashcroft Arts Centre later this year. ​ I’m pleased to have supported this initiative last year and to have worked with Hampshire County Council and the team to help move this forward. ​ This will be a great community asset! ​ Watch my video on Facebook here! - https://www.facebook.com/share/r/1KeUKiJTC5/ Fix the potholes! 30th March 2026 ​ I’ll keep urging Hampshire County Council to fix the potholes. ​ Several residents have complained to me about the state of roads in different parts of Fareham & Waterlooville, like London Road in Waterlooville and Wynton Way in Fareham where I saw loads recently. ​ You pay your Council Tax and deserve better! ​ ​ Scrap the Family Farm Tax 27th March 2026 ​ Good meeting today at Rookesbury Park with the Country Land and Business Association and local farmers from Fareham and Waterlooville. It was a valuable opportunity to listen to those who know our countryside best and understand the realities of running farms and rural businesses day to day. ​ We spoke about the challenges facing British farming from rising costs and regulation to the need for long‑term investment and sustainable food production. Our farmers work tirelessly to feed the nation, care for the land, and support local jobs, and their contributions deserve both recognition and support. ​ I’ll continue working with local landowners and the CLA to champion rural communities, protect our farmland, and ensure British agriculture remains at the heart of a strong, self‑reliant nation. ​ ​ ​ Ending DEI at the FA 24th March 2026 The Football Association wants to mandate that 1 in 4 football coaches come from a Black, Asian or other minority background. As the saying in football goes, this is utter woke nonsense. The game’s gone. ​ F

pages.title: About | Suella Braverman
pages.h1: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> About | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact About Suella Suella is very proud to be the Reform UK Member of Parliament for Fareham and Waterlooville.​ She was first elected as the MP for Fareham in 2015 and was subsequently re-elected in 2017 and 2019. In 2024, the boundaries for the constituency changed, and Suella was re-elected for the new seat of Fareham and Waterlooville.​ She has consistently championed policies that promote free speech, support hard work and supported and secure borders. ​ From 2018 to 2020, she served as a Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State at the Department for Exiting the European Union, playing a role in delivering the UK’s departure from the European Union. ​ She was appointed Attorney General for England and Wales and Advocate General for Northern Ireland in 2020, serving as the Government’s chief legal adviser. In this role, she provided legal counsel to the Government ​ From 2022-2023 Suella served as Home Secretary, one of the great offices of state, leading work on immigration reform, policing, crime reduction, and national security. She focused on strengthening border controls, supporting law enforcement, and enhancing public safety for the British people ​ As MP for Fareham and Waterlooville, Suella works closely with local residents, businesses, schools, and community organisations. She regularly holds surgeries, meets with local groups, and campaigns on issues important to the constituency, including infrastructure improvements, healthcare provision, education, andkeeping local people safe. ​ Suella is married with two children and lives locally in Hampshire. She remains committed to public service and to representing the interests of her constituents of Fareham and Waterlooville in Parliament. ​ Suella help thousands of constituents every year with a variety of different problems and issues. If she is your MP and you think you need her help, please contact her email – suella.braverman.mp@parliament.uk . Is Suella your MP © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy bottom of page

pages.title: Campaigns | Suella Braverman
pages.h1: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> Campaigns | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Fareham and Waterlooville I am very proud to be the Reform UK Member of Parliament for Fareham and Waterlooville. As well as supporting individual constituents to solve their issues and problems I have also has run many successful campaigns on local and national issues, a selection of these are below where you can find out more. If you think I can help you on any of these matters or something else please email suella.braverman.mp@parliament.uk Waterlooville Regeneration Havant Borough Council has promised that a regeneration of Waterlooville town centre is coming. So where is it? I am urging HBC to get on with the job and deliver for local people. Supporting our farmers Suella is very proud to support our brilliant British farmers. She voted against the Family Farm Tax and will continue to speak up for the countryside. Southern Water Suella is passionate about holding Southern Water to account. In January 2026 she held a public meeting with water executives getting people to have their say. Learn more Support our farms Southern Water Anti-social behaviour Suella is committed to working with Hampshire & Isle of Wight Police as well as local councils and community groups to keep Fareham and Waterlooville safe. London Road, Waterlooville Suella led the successful campaign against the Home Office placing illegal migrants in the heart of Waterlooville. Her petition was signed by over 10,000 people. Portchester banking hub Suella has been campaigning for a Banking Hub to be put into Portchester. She created a petition and continues to speak on this issue in Parliament. Learn more Our campaign Support me Henry Cort College & SEND SEND is not working for so many families it's too bureaucratic, too expensive and insufficient resource for our children and families. It’s also why I keep urging Hampshire County Council to repurpose Henry Cort College as a Special Education Needs School. Fareham Boxing Club Suella is actively supporting Fareham Boxing Club and their search for a permanent home. She recognises their positive role in youth development, fitness and community safety. Rowans Hospice Suella has championed Rowans Hospice as an indispensable community resource and campaigned for successfully for increased funding and recognition of the vital care it provides to families facing life-limiting illnesses. Learn more Help FBC Rowans © 2026 Promoted by Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy bottom of page

pages.title: Contact | Suella Braverman
pages.h1: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
pages.text_excerpt: --> Contact | Suella Braverman top of page Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP Home News About Campaigns Articles Events Contact The best way to get in touch with Suella is by emailing suella.braverman.mp@parliament.uk ​ As the Member of Parliament for Fareham & Waterlooville, Suella is only able to assist residents who live within the constituency. To help us respond to your enquiry, please ensure you include your full home address and a contact telephone number in your message. If these details were not provided, kindly resend your email with the missing information. ​ If you are unsure whether she is your MP, you can check by using the “Find MPs” tool on the UK Parliament website. ​ Due to the high volume of correspondence received, the office aims to respond to all constituency-related enquiries within 10 working days. ​ Please note that if your message is part of a coordinated campaign or a standard email drafted by a third party, its contents will be carefully noted; however, an individual response may not be issued. Responses to major campaigns are regularly published on the website.​ Constituents who wish to receive the monthly newsletter are welcome to subscribe via this website. ​ If you would like Suella to attend an event you are hosting, please include as much information as possible. ​ Thank you again for making contact and we will be happy to help. © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy © 2026 Promoted by the Office of Suella Braverman KC MP Home Contact Cookie Policy bottom of page
